blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
Here are the best real, existing Android apps available on the Google Play Store for playing **Blind Man's Bluff** (also commonly known as **Indian Poker**):

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game** (by hheeee)
* **Overview:** This is a highly-rated, single-player psychological card game directly based on the rules of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **How it works:** Both you and an AI opponent receive a card face-down. You cannot see your own card, but you can see the AI's card. You must read the AI's facial expressions and betting patterns to guess your card's value. 
* **Key Features:** 
  * Three AI difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, Hard) with distinct psychological behaviors.
  * Strategic betting options: CALL, HALF, DIE, or ALL-IN.
  * Avatar customization (hairstyles, outfits) and unlockable card deck designs.
  * 100% offline play with no Wi-Fi or account required.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Overview:** A multiplayer-focused adaptation of the classic forehead card game.
* **How it works:** Designed to be played with friends and family, this app lets you host games where you must predict your victory by looking only at your opponent's card. 
* **Key Features:**
  * Clean, straightforward interface.
  * Focuses heavily on the psychological aspect of reading your friends' real-time expressions.
  * Great for casual local gatherings or family game nights.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Overview:** A lightweight, simple simulation app designed to facilitate the game in a group setting.
* **How it works:** It acts as a digital dealer and card-holder for multiple players. 
* **Key Features:**
  * Minimalist, ad-supported, and very low on storage space.
  * Best if you want a no-frills, quick setup to play the game with a group of people using your devices.

forehead poker app Android.response:
If you are looking to play **Forehead Poker** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Blind Man's Bluff**—where you can see everyone else's cards but your own), there are several great options available on the Google Play Store. 

Here are the best Android apps for playing this classic psychological card game, along with a popular party alternative:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Best for:** Single-player and offline play.
* **Description:** This is a highly polished, single-player card game based entirely on the rules of Blind Man's Bluff (Indian Poker). You are dealt a card face-down that only the AI opponent can see, while the AI's card is shown face-up to you. You have to read the AI's "expressions" and betting patterns to guess the value of your card and decide whether to Call, Fold (Die), Raise (Half), or go All-In. It features multiple difficulty levels and customizable avatars.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Best for:** Playing with friends and family locally.
* **Description:** This app is designed to let you play the classic forehead poker game with people around you. It acts as a digital deck where players can pass the phone or look at the screen to see their opponents' cards while keeping their own hidden. It is a great, lightweight option for party settings to test your psychological bluffing skills.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Best for:** A simple, no-frills multiplayer setup.
* **Description:** A straightforward simulation app designed for multiple people to play Indian Poker together. It provides a clean digital interface to replace a physical deck of cards, making it easy to play the game anywhere without needing to carry a physical pack.

---

### *Alternative "Forehead" Party Game:*
If you actually meant the popular **word-guessing party game** where you hold the phone to your forehead, you should download:

* **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
  * **Description:** The ultimate party game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. You place the phone on your forehead, and your friends try to describe the word on the screen (celebrities, movies, animals, etc.) before the timer runs out.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
If you are looking for a card game where you cannot see your own cards, there are two main types of games with this mechanic: **Hanabi** (a cooperative game where you work with other players) and **Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff** (a competitive betting game where you hold your card to your forehead). 

The following real, existing Android apps on the Google Play Store feature this unique mechanic:

### 1. Hanabi Apps (Cooperative)
In Hanabi, players work together to launch a spectacular fireworks show. The twist is that you hold your cards facing outward—you can see everyone else's cards, but you cannot see your own. You must rely on clues from your teammates to know what to play.

*   **Hanabi Online** (by Ordon Team): A dedicated, free Android app that allows you to play the classic cooperative card game online with players from around the world or with your friends. 
*   **Boardible - Board Games** (by Boardible): A digital board game platform available on Android that features a highly-rated, fully playable digital version of *Hanabi*. It is designed to make playing with friends online or locally very smooth.
*   **Hanabi Tracker** (by André Rhine-Davis): If you already own the physical card game, this is a highly rated utility app. Since you can't see your own cards, this app helps you easily track the clues and negative information your teammates have given you throughout the game.

### 2. Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff Apps (Competitive)
In this classic party/betting game, each player is dealt a card that they must hold to their forehead (or is displayed on-screen facing away from them). You can see everyone else's cards, but not your own, and you must bet on whether you have the highest card based on your opponents' reactions and bets.

*   **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.): This app lets you play Indian Poker on your mobile device. You predict your victory by looking only at your opponent's card and trying to read their facial expressions and betting patterns.
*   **Indian poker** (by Kanbe): A straightforward, multiplayer-focused simulation app designed to let you play Indian Poker with friends using your smartphones.
*   **Blindex**: A free, offline single-player Blind Man's Bluff (Indian Poker) app. It allows you to play head-to-head against an AI dealer with multiple difficulty levels, meaning you don't need an internet connection to play.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The game you are thinking of is commonly referred to as a **"Heads Up"** or **"Charades"** style game. In these games, one player holds the phone to their forehead with the screen facing outward, while other players act out, describe, or mimic the word on the screen so the player can guess it. 

The best and most popular Android apps for this game on the Google Play Store include:

### 1. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
This is the official, smash-hit game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. It is widely considered the gold standard for this genre.
* **How it works:** You select a deck (such as Celebrities, Movies, Animals, or custom decks), place the phone on your forehead, and try to guess the words based on your friends' clues. 
* **Key Feature:** It uses your phone's front camera to record video of your friends frantically acting out the clues, which you can save and watch later.
* *Note: There is also a **Heads Up! Netflix Edition** available for free if you have an active Netflix subscription.*

### 2. **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** (by Cosmicode)
A highly-rated, feature-rich alternative that offers a polished experience.
* **How it works:** Classic reverse-charades gameplay where you hold the phone to your forehead.
* **Key Feature:** It includes a "Team Mode" to easily keep score during competitive game nights, supports 26 languages, and allows you to create your own custom categories.

### 3. **Charades!** (by FatChicken Studios)
One of the oldest and most popular free alternatives on the Play Store.
* **How it works:** It features a clean, simple interface where you tilt the phone down for a correct guess and tilt it up to pass/skip.
* **Key Feature:** It offers a massive variety of free categories (ranging from 80s music to active video game trivia) and supports multiple teams for large gatherings.

### 4. **ForeHeads: Charades Guess Game**
A great, family-friendly option designed specifically to mimic the classic "headbands" board game.
* **How it works:** Hold the phone to your forehead and guess the word before the 60-second timer runs out.
* **Key Feature:** It features over 48 unique decks and 6,500+ challenges, with a focus on clean, ad-free gameplay options.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best real, existing single-player, offline bluffing card games available on Android:

### 1. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
* **Description:** This is a dedicated digital adaptation of the classic card game "Cheat" (also widely known as "I Doubt It," "Bluff," or "BS"). It features a robust offline single-player mode where you go head-to-head against three AI opponents. You play cards face-down and declare their rank, deciding whether to tell the truth or bluff. If the AI calls your bluff, you take the penalty pile; if you catch them lying, they do. The app also introduces fun strategic items like "Lie Counters" and "Bluff Shields" to keep the gameplay fresh.

### 2. **Bluff Card Game Offline**
* **Description:** Another excellent offline version of the classic "I Doubt It" card game. It is designed specifically to be played anytime without an internet connection. It features smart AI opponents with dynamic difficulty, offering both a quick 2-player (1v1) mode and a full 4-player table mode. It has smooth card animations, daily quests, and clean UI, making it a highly reliable offline option.

### 3. **Coup** *(by Banana & Co.)*
* **Description:** A digital port of the highly popular tabletop social deduction and bluffing card game. While it supports online multiplayer, it includes a fully offline single-player mode where you play against AI opponents. The goal is to manipulate, bluff, and claim the powers of cards you may or may not actually hold (like the Assassin, Captain, or Duke) to eliminate your opponents' influence and be the last player standing.

### 4. **Love Letter - Card Game** *(by Twin Sails Interactive)*
* **Description:** An official digital adaptation of the award-winning micro-card game of deduction, risk, and bluffing. It features a dedicated single-player mode where you can play offline against computer-controlled AI. With a hand of only one or two cards, you must strategically play character abilities to gain information, protect your hand, and bluff your opponents into making mistakes to successfully deliver your letter to the princess.

### 5. **Underhand** *(by Spoopy Squad)*
* **Description:** A cult-classic, atmospheric single-player card game designed to be played entirely offline. While not a traditional "poker-face" bluffing game against other players, it is a resource-management card game where you play as a cult leader. You must constantly make high-stakes, risky decisions and "bluff" your way through dangerous event cards (like police raids and cultist uprisings) by managing your hand of resources to successfully summon ancient deities.

best offline single player card game And.response:
Here are some of the best offline, single-player card games available on the Google Play Store, split into modern deckbuilders/RPGs and classic card games:

### **Roguelike Deckbuilders & RPG Card Games**

*   **Slay the Spire**
    *   **Description:** Widely considered the gold standard of roguelike deckbuilders. You choose one of four unique characters and attempt to climb a procedurally generated spire. Along the way, you draft cards, fight bizarre monsters, and collect powerful relics that synergize with your deck. It is incredibly deep, highly replayable, and fully playable offline.
*   **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
    *   **Description:** A premium, portrait-oriented deckbuilder designed specifically for mobile play. It features over 900 hand-illustrated cards and multiple distinct classes (like Rogue, Warrior, and Seeker). It is a complete game with no microtransactions or paywalls, offering a deep, story-driven RPG experience.
*   **Night of the Full Moon**
    *   **Description:** Based on a dark retelling of the Little Red Riding Hood fairy tale, this game combines roguelike exploration with card combat. You choose a profession (such as Nun, Ranger, or Knight) and build a deck as you explore the Black Forest. It features beautiful art, multiple difficulty levels, and a highly engaging offline campaign.
*   **Card Thief**
    *   **Description:** A unique stealth-puzzle game played on a 3x3 grid of cards. You play as a thief sneaking through the shadows, extinguishing torches, pickpocketing guards, and stealing treasure. card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The card game you are describing is **Hanabi** (also known as **Hanab**), and the Android app is **Hanabi** by **Playdek** (or simply search for "Hanabi" on Google Play).

In **Hanabi**, players cannot see their own cards; instead, they hold their cards facing outward so other players can see them, and teammates give clues about the cards to help the player place them correctly in a sequence [1]. The game is a cooperative puzzle where the goal is to build fireworks displays of numbers 1–5 in each color [1].

**Recommended Android App:**
- **App Name:** **Hanabi**  
- **Developer:** **Playdek** (or available via Playdek’s card game suite)  
- **Description:** A faithful digital adaptation of the physical card game where you and your team work together to build fireworks without seeing your own cards.  
- **Availability:** Available on Google Play Store for Android.

If the Playdek version is unavailable, you can also search for **"Hanabi Card Game"** on Google Play, as multiple developers have released versions of this game [1].

Other similar games mentioned in discussions where you can’t see your own hand include **Skyjo** (commercial version called "Skyjo") and **Port Royal**, but **Hanabi** is the most iconic and widely played cooperative card game with this specific mechanic [1].

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The Android game you’re describing is **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros.), where one player holds the phone to their forehead with the screen facing out, and friends give clues to guess the word displayed on the screen [3][8].

Another closely matching option is **Charades & Headbands Game** (by Net Art Game Studio), which explicitly says: “Place device on forehead… guess what is on your device!” and involves miming, singing, or giving clues [2].

A third possibility is the official **Hedbanz app**, based on the physical card game where players wear a headband and insert a card facing out so others can see it, then guess what’s on their head using yes/no questions [1].

For the most popular and widely used app that fits “holding the phone to your forehead,” **Heads Up!** is the best recommendation [3][8].

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
No single-player, offline **Bluff** card game exists for Android; the popular **Bluff** card game (also known as *Cheat* or *Open*) is strictly a **multiplayer** game requiring online opponents to call out lies [1][3].

However, for **single-player**, **offline** Android card games that feature **bluffing** or strategic deception mechanics, here are the best real app recommendations:

| App Name | Why It Fits Your Request |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Night of the Full Moon** | A vertical, **offline**, single-player roguelite deck-builder where you bluff and strategize against enemies; microtransactions are optional and unnecessary to beat the hardest settings [2]. |
| **Void Tyrant** | A vertical, **offline**, single-player deck-building roguelite with character progression and strategic bluffing elements [2]. |
| **Card Quest** | A single-player, **offline** deck-building adventure with resource management and strategic card play [2]. |
| **Gwent: The Witcher Card Game** | While it has online modes, its core mechanic is **bluffing** (placing cards to outsmart foes); check if a single-player mode is available in the current version [5]. |
| **Slay the Spire** | The top **single-player**, **offline** roguelike deck-builder where you blend card strategy with endless adventures (no bluffing, but high strategic depth) [5]. |

If you specifically want the classic **Bluff** game mechanics (announcing a rank face-down and calling "Open!"), you must play with friends or online players, as the game logic requires an opponent to challenge your lie [1][3]. For a classic **offline** single-player card game, **Microsoft Solitaire Collection** is the standard recommendation [5].
